# Implementation Plan: Add Observability (OpenTelemetry)

**Branch**: `001-add-observability` | **Date**: 2026-03-25 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-add-observability/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Add opt-in OpenTelemetry observability to MXC so that operators and developers can measure
product adoption and diagnose performance issues without gathering any personally identifiable
information.

**Rust layer**: A new `wxc_common::telemetry` module (`src/wxc_common/src/telemetry.rs`) wraps
the `tracing` + `tracing-opentelemetry` + `opentelemetry_sdk` crate stack. Both `wxc-exec` and
`lxc-exec` binaries call `telemetry::init()` at startup and `telemetry::shutdown()` before
exit. Telemetry is off by default; it activates only when `MXC_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1` or
`"telemetry": { "enabled": true }` is present in the JSON config file.

**TypeScript layer**: A new `sdk/src/telemetry.ts` module wraps
`@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node` + `@opentelemetry/sdk-metrics`. When the SDK spawns a child
process it injects the W3C `TRACEPARENT` env var so Rust child spans attach to the SDK-layer
parent span, forming a single distributed trace across process boundaries.

**Span / metric schema**: Research decision R6. Root span `mxc.execute` → five child
spans (`mxc.container.init`, `mxc.policy.filesystem`, `mxc.policy.network`, `mxc.script.run`,
`mxc.container.teardown`). Three metric instruments: `mxc.executions` (Counter),
`mxc.failures` (Counter), `mxc.execution.duration` (Histogram).

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Rust stable (1.78+), TypeScript 5.x / Node.js 20+  
**Primary Dependencies**:
  - Rust (new): `opentelemetry 0.31`, `opentelemetry_sdk 0.31`, `opentelemetry-otlp 0.31 (http-proto)`,
    `tracing 0.1`, `tracing-opentelemetry 0.32`, `tracing-subscriber 0.3`,
    `opentelemetry-semantic-conventions 0.31`
  - TypeScript (new): `@opentelemetry/api ^1.9`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node ^1.29`,
    `@opentelemetry/sdk-metrics ^1.29`, `@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http ^0.57`,
    `@opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-http ^0.57`
  - Existing: `serde`, `serde_json`, `tokio`, `thiserror`, `anyhow`, `windows-sys`

**Storage**: N/A — telemetry emitted over OTLP HTTP; no local file storage  
**Testing**: `cargo test --release` (Rust unit tests in `#[cfg(test)]` modules); `npm test` (SDK/CLI)  
**Target Platform**: Windows 11 x64/ARM64 (wxc-exec); Linux x64/ARM64 (lxc-exec)  
**Project Type**: Library (`wxc_common`) + two CLI binaries + TypeScript SDK/CLI  
**Performance Goals**: Telemetry overhead ≤ 5 ms per execution (FR-016); async-only exporter;
  no synchronous flush on the hot path  
**Constraints**: No default remote endpoint (OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT must be set
  explicitly); telemetry off by default (opt-in); force-flush + shutdown ≤ 2 seconds on exit
  (FR-017); zero PII in spans or metrics  
**Scale/Scope**: Two Rust binaries + one TypeScript SDK + one TypeScript CLI; no additional
  crates required beyond workspace additions to `src/Cargo.toml`

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

- [x] **I. Codebase Conventions**: All new files (`telemetry.rs`, `sdk/src/telemetry.ts`) will
  carry the Microsoft copyright header following the pattern in existing files. New deps are
  added to `[workspace.dependencies]` in `src/Cargo.toml` and referenced via `{ workspace = true }`
  in crate `Cargo.toml` files — no deviation from workspace dep pattern. Node deps added to
  `sdk/package.json` and `cli/package.json`.
- [x] **II. Test-First**: Task list (to be generated by `/speckit.tasks`) orders unit tests for
  `telemetry::is_enabled`, `telemetry::init` (no-op path), and `TelemetryConfig::default` before
  any implementation task. Integration test for force-flush behavior precedes the flush
  implementation task.
- [x] **III. Rust Best Practices**: `telemetry.rs` lives in `wxc_common` (library crate) and
  returns `Option<TelemetryHandles>` — telemetry init/shutdown errors are intentionally
  swallowed per FR-008 (failures MUST NOT surface to callers). `anyhow` is used only in
  `wxc/src/main.rs` and `lxc/src/main.rs` (binary entry points). `TelemetryConfig` derives
  `serde::Deserialize` with `#[serde(default)]`. The telemetry exporter task spawns on the
  existing `tokio` runtime — no second runtime created.
- [x] **IV. OpenTelemetry Observability**: Spec FR-001 through FR-006 define the span names
  and mandatory attributes. `data-model.md` documents the full span hierarchy and metric
  instruments. Instrumentation tasks cover both Rust (`tracing::info_span!`) and TypeScript
  (`tracer.startSpan`) layers.
- [x] **V. Security by Design**: `"telemetry"` JSON section is parsed through the same
  `RawConfig` deserialization path as all other sections — `RawTelemetry.enabled` is
  `Option<bool>` (only valid values accepted by `serde`; no free-form string). Spec FR-015
  prohibits PII in telemetry; data-model bounded-enum dimensions enforce this at the type
  level. No new container capability or policy bypass is introduced.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-add-observability/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output — Rust/TS crate selection, span schema, no-op path
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output — TelemetryConfig struct, span hierarchy, metrics
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output — end-to-end validation with local OTel collector
├── contracts/
│   └── json-config-telemetry.md  # JSON schema, TS interface, env var contract
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command — NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── Cargo.toml                       # MODIFY — add OTel workspace deps
├── wxc_common/
│   └── src/
│       ├── telemetry.rs             # NEW — init/shutdown/is_enabled, TelemetryConfig, TelemetryHandles
│       ├── models.rs                # MODIFY — add TelemetryConfig field to CodexRequest
│       ├── config_parser.rs         # MODIFY — add RawTelemetry, parse into TelemetryConfig
│       └── lib.rs                   # MODIFY — pub mod telemetry;
├── wxc/
│   └── src/
│       └── main.rs                  # MODIFY — init telemetry, wrap execution span, shutdown
└── lxc/
    └── src/
        └── main.rs                  # MODIFY — init telemetry, wrap execution span, shutdown

sdk/
├── package.json                     # MODIFY — add @opentelemetry/* deps
└── src/
    ├── telemetry.ts                 # NEW — initTelemetry, shutdownTelemetry, getTraceParent
    ├── types.ts                     # MODIFY — add WxcTelemetryConfig interface, add telemetry?: field
    └── index.ts                     # MODIFY — export telemetry helpers, pass TRACEPARENT to child env

cli/
├── package.json                     # MODIFY — add @opentelemetry/* deps
└── src/
    └── cli.ts                       # MODIFY — honour MXC_ENABLE_TELEMETRY flag; init/shutdown

Readme.md                            # MODIFY — add Telemetry section with opt-in instructions
                                     #          and privacy disclosure (FR-015, SC-007)
```

**Structure Decision**: Single Rust workspace + TypeScript monorepo (packages under `sdk/` and
`cli/`). No new Rust crates are required — telemetry logic lives in `wxc_common` alongside the
existing `models`, `config_parser`, `logger`, and `script_runner` modules. This matches the
existing pattern and avoids workspace churn.
